element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used throughout all levels; Western version replaces headbutt with pan smash
Early ranged option; placement moved rightward in Western version to increase difficulty
Added in Western version near extra life pickup
Mid-game ranged option; repositioned or removed in Western version
Western version exclusive; powerful spinning attack; replaces Japanese version's black frying pan variant
Basic health recovery; placement significantly altered across regional versions to adjust difficulty
Extended health gauge; Western version removes some, replaces others with health candy
Risk/reward placement; Meat level requires risky jump off platform edge
Apple enemies (Soup), Toaster enemies (Salad), Bubble vats (Salad)

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe platform after vertical scrolling section
typical_tension_triggers: Twin conveyor belts with tight spacing (Salad)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Early levels establish navigation and basic combat, middle levels introduce environmental hazards and precision platforming, late levels stack multiple hazards and require weapon mastery; Western version intensifies difficulty curve through item placement repositioning

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Extra life and health pickups often positioned off main path requiring deliberate jumps or risky maneuvers; Meat level extra life requires jumping off platform edge
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Alternating platform patterns and conveyor belt sequences signal optional/challenging pickups; health items clustered before boss areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Appetizer introduces basic navigation and weapon collection on slopes and simple platforms; first building exterior teaches spoon weapon placement; interior restaurant funnels player through giant doors to boss
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Soup and Salad levels introduce vertical movement sections, multiple enemy types (apples, toasters, bubbles), and conveyor belt puzzle-platforming; health resource management increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fish and Meat levels stack vertical scrolling with falling platforms and hook mechanics; Dessert combines waterfall navigation with tight jump requirements; final corridor gauntlet before Ohdove
notable_spikes: Appetizer downward slope with repositioned spoon weapon (Western version)
Salad level conveyor belt sequences with twin belts and tight platform spacing
Meat level falling ice platform sections
Dessert final corridor with relocated health-extending lollipop requiring risky jump
Boss corridors with health candy placement changes (Western version removes some items entirely)

notable_level_patterns.description: Six levels named after meal courses (Appetizer, Soup, Salad, Fish, Meat, Dessert) with thematic enemy/obstacle design; creates menu progression metaphor
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Salad level features bubble vats and toaster enemies; Dessert features waterfall sections

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level exterior leads to restaurant interior with giant doors marking boss entrance; interior segments tighten challenge and signal final confrontation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Appetizer level places health candy between giant doors before boss

notable_level_patterns.description: Fish, Meat, and Dessert levels feature multiple vertical climbing/falling sections with environmental hazards (ice platforms, hooks, waterfalls)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Meat level removes health items from vertical sections in Western version, increasing tension

notable_level_patterns.description: Western version systematically repositions weapons to increase collection difficulty; spoons moved rightward, forks added near jumps, plates replaced with spoons
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Salad spoon positioned above toaster enemy; Soup spoon shifted right

notable_level_patterns.description: Japanese version includes health-extending lollipops; Western version replaces many with standard health candy or removes entirely, forcing more conservative play
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Appetizer removes lollipop and plate weapon inside restaurant (Western only)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Panic Restaurant (stage layout regional differences)
Wikipedia - Panic Restaurant (game mechanics, character/weapon changes, designer credit)
